key_drivers: The broader dollar backdrop remains supportive because DXY was only marginally higher on the day near 98.287 but still lower over the one-month horizon, preserving a weak-dollar cross-asset tailwind.

Policy divergence now matters more than outright Fed easing: FedWatch showed 99.5% pricing for no change at the next FOMC meeting, while the ECB remains on a hawkish hold, the RBA stays overtly hawkish after its March hike, and BoJ normalization still supports yen strength.

Risk conditions are constructive rather than euphoric because VIX stayed balanced at 17.48 and fell on the day, while recent repository-verified MOVE and SOX trends still favor selective equity leadership led by Nasdaq rather than broad small-cap beta.

Crude retains the cleanest commodity trend because the latest verified official EIA backdrop still points to tightening supply and a crude draw, whereas stale but still negative March gold-ETF flow evidence caps precious-metals conviction.

stale_sources: Atlanta Fed GDPNow remains the April 9, 2026 official update at 1.3% for 2026:Q1.
FRED DFII10 and BAMLH0A0HYM2 latest official observations lag the report timestamp by normal publication delay.
World Gold Council ETF-flow evidence remains anchored to the March 2026 release.
fallbacks_used: The most recent repository-verified scoring detail from 2026-04-18 was retained for oil inventories, oil supply, MOVE, SOX, copper, eurozone growth, and gold-ETF classification where direct same-session extraction was incomplete.
Direct official browser review confirmed the March 18-19 ECB hold, the March 17 RBA 25 bp hike to 4.10%, the March 19 BoJ policy statement, and the March China PMI rebound to 50.4.
The visible CME FedWatch probability table was used directly for the next-meeting Fed classification because the full probability table remained only partially machine-readable.
overnight_changes: DXY was slightly firmer on the day but remains weaker over the broader monthly lookback.
VIX closed in the balanced band and fell from the prior close, while recent MOVE readings remain lower.
Real yields and HY OAS both ticked slightly higher in the latest FRED prints, while the 2s10s curve steepened modestly.

imminent: Atlanta Fed GDPNow update on April 21, 2026
near_term: BoJ normalization communication remains an active FX catalyst
background: Ongoing Gulf shipping and energy-supply headlines
